summary: This is a brief news post with two items. First, Joel shares a podcast interview with Dan Bricklin (co-creator of VisiCalc) covering topics like GPL compliance, open source economics, and copyright awareness among developers. Second, Joel shares a short anecdote about being warned about a Wall Street bond scam by his friend Jared, only to fall for it himself shortly after.
key_points: Joel discussed GPL compliance and the importance of developers tracking where their code comes from in an interview with Dan Bricklin
He argued that developers who read Slashdot and Eric Raymond are more copyright-aware than their managers
Joel shared a self-deprecating story about falling for a Wall Street bond scam despite having it explained to him beforehand
money_quotes: developers who read Slashdot and Eric Raymond are more likely to be at least somewhat aware of copyright issues than their managers who don't
Remember that scam I explained to you last month?
Well, you just fell for it!
key_insight: This is a lightweight news post linking to external content rather than making a substantive argument, though it touches on the theme that understanding a risk intellectually doesn't prevent you from falling victim to it.
